Composition.Programmer 1:
Fix and complete the backend Python application (app.py) for the 'TravelPlanner' web application. This includes 
- Ensuring the Flask app instance is properly created and named 'app' in 'app.py' to avoid import and runtime issues.
- Implementing routing for all frontend pages as per the original task, with the first page accessible at route '/' (Dashboard).
- Handling file read and write operations from the 'data/' directory using plain text files as specified.
- Correcting all syntax, logic, and structural errors in the current backend code to ensure full functionality and compliance with the requirements.
- Supporting all frontend pages  dashboard.html, destinations.html, destination_details.html, itinerary_planning.html, accommodations.html, transportation.html, travel_packages.html, trip_management.html, booking_confirmation.html, travel_recommendations.html.
- Incorporating the original task's supplementary condition  no authentication required; all features directly accessible.
Composition.Programmer 2:
Develop all frontend HTML pages for the 'TravelPlanner' web application. This includes 
- Completing the frontend code for all pages listed in the coding plan.
- Ensuring all URLs in the frontend code use backend routes (not direct HTML file names).
- Implementing the page elements and IDs exactly as specified in the requirements document.
- Ensuring the website starts from the Dashboard page route '/'.
- Coordinating with Programmer 1 to align frontend navigation and element IDs with backend routes and data structures.

Composition.Programmer 1:
Test whether the website can be accessed through local port 5000. In this test, you only need to test whether the first page of the website can be accessed through the local 5000 port.
Task 2  Test whether the first page of the website loads correctly and whether basic navigation works based on the example data provided in the Task.
Task 3  Test the elements and integrity of all pages. This includes verifying the presence and correctness of all specified elements on each page as per the requirements

background_prompt: EvoMAC is a software company powered by multiple intelligent agents, such as chief executive officer, chief human resources officer, chief product officer, chief technology officer, etc, with a multi-agent organizational structure and the mission of 'changing the digital world through programming'.
